title: Cylindrolobus lohitensis
taxonomy: Cylindrolobus lohitensis is classified as a species of fungi in the genus Cylindrolobus, which belongs to the family Phanerochaetaceae. This classification places it within the larger group of Ascomycota, a division of fungi characterized by the presence of ascospores. The genus Cylindrolobus is known for its unique characteristics, including the production of cylindrical ascocarps. At the species level, Cylindrolobus lohitensis is a relatively recently discovered species. Further research is needed to fully understand its classification and relationships to other fungal species.

intro: Cylindrolobus lohitensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae, native to the eastern Himalayas and parts of China. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, contributing to its potential health benefits in treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Cylindrolobus lohitensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Cylindrolobus lohitensis is commonly prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions to harness its therapeutic benefits.
